title: Box office: Civil War wins 2nd weekend, besting vampire flick Abigail
text: Civil War won the second weekend box office race, earning $11 million while Abigail opened with $10.2 million domestically. The close competition highlighted a crowded marketplace for adult-oriented films during Easter weekend. Civil War's strong retention in its second weekend (a 57% decline from its record $25.7 million opening) demonstrated the strength of Alex Garland's dystopian thriller. Abigail, with a global opening of $15.2 million ($10.2 million domestic, $5.2 million international), opened below the industry projection of $12-15 million. Universal's vampire horror film, directed by Radio Silence (Matt Bettinelli-Olpin and Tyler Gillett), benefited from critical acclaim with an 82% Rotten Tomatoes score but received a B CinemaScore from audiences. The $28 million budget film required approximately $56-70 million worldwide to break even with marketing and distribution costs included. The underwhelming opening marked the latest in a series of horror underperformances in 2024, leading industry observers to question the genre's theatrical viability. Despite strong critical reviews and a talented cast featuring Melissa Barrera and Dan Stevens, the film struggled to convert critical praise into box office success.
